title: Visa Opens the Door to AI-Driven Shopping for Businesses Worldwide
text: Visa unveiled Intelligent Commerce Connect, a solution in the Visa Intelligent Commerce portfolio designed to help agent builders, merchants, and payment enablers participate in AI-powered commerce through a single integration. The product works through the Visa Acceptance Platform and is intended to support secure payment initiation, tokenization, spend controls, and authentication for agentic transactions. Visa said the pilot includes partners such as Aldar, AWS, Diddo, Highnote, Mesh, Payabli, and Sumvin, with broader rollout planned during the year. The announcement matters for stock analysis because it connects Visa's existing acceptance infrastructure to a possible new wave of AI-assisted shopping, where software agents discover products and initiate purchases for consumers. If adoption grows, Visa could defend and extend its role in digital checkout, tokenization, merchant acceptance, and value-added services. The main uncertainty is whether agentic commerce becomes mainstream quickly enough to create material transaction volume and revenue.
